example_prompt: Add an Orange Money Mali webhook handler at the URL passed as notif_url in create_payment. Authenticate the inbound POST by matching the notif_token field against the value previously stored when the payment was created, then mark the order as paid only when status === 'SUCCESS'.

input_schema.description: Payload Orange Money POSTs (Content-Type: application/json) to your notif_url when a transaction reaches a final state. Field names follow the Orange Money Web Payment v1 contract.

status.description: Final transaction status. Known values include: 'SUCCESS' (paid), 'FAILED' (rejected by user or operator), 'EXPIRED' (timeout). Treat anything other than 'SUCCESS' as not-paid.

txnid.description: Orange Money transaction identifier — useful for support and reconciliation.

pay_token.description: The pay_token returned by create_payment for this session. Use it to look up the order in your database.

notif_token.description: The notif_token returned by create_payment for this session. MUST match the value you persisted at creation time — this is the only way to authenticate the webhook (Orange Money does not sign the payload otherwise).

order_id.description: The order_id you supplied to create_payment. Echoed back so you can locate the order without joining on pay_token.

amount.description: Amount that was authorised, in the smallest currency unit. May be returned as a string depending on the country/version — coerce defensively.

reference.description: Free-text reference you supplied to create_payment, echoed back.

response_schema.description: Your endpoint must return HTTP 200 within a few seconds. The response body is ignored. A non-2xx response or a timeout means the notification is lost — Orange Money does not retry.

gotchas: The ONLY authentication mechanism is matching the inbound notif_token against the value you stored when create_payment returned. Reject any POST whose notif_token does not match a known session — anyone who guesses your URL can otherwise POST to it.

Return HTTP 200 immediately, before doing any business work. Orange Money does NOT retry a failed delivery — if your handler times out (typically after a few seconds) or throws, the notification is permanently lost and you must reconcile manually.

Treat status === 'SUCCESS' as the only paid state. 'FAILED' / 'EXPIRED' (and any other value) mean the customer was not charged. Do not rely on the mere fact of receiving a webhook to fulfill the order.

The exact set of fields and casing in the JSON payload may vary across Orange Money country deployments and API versions. Code defensively: validate notif_token first, then read fields with optional chaining and explicit type coercion (e.g. amount may be a string).

Orange Money never calls back the return_url server-side — the user's browser is just redirected there. Order fulfillment MUST happen from this webhook handler (or a polled status check), never from the return_url request.
